mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in rate-cut months are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.8%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth, prompting the Federal Reserve to hike policy rates and compress equity multiples, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive or neutral when UNRATE drops below 4.8% for two consecutive months, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer credit expansion measured by RSAFS combined with rising industrial production INDPRO signals robust aggregate demand outpacing supply constraints, driving corporate earnings growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint RSAFS and INDPRO signal produces negative or zero predictive power for 60-day forward returns in walk-forward tests across any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, housing starts HOUST act as a leading indicator of consumer confidence; a sustained decline in HOUST during Fed tightening predicts a sharp equity drawdown as mortgage rates crowd out discretionary spending.
failure_condition: HOUST declines during Fed hikes do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or the signal's Sharpe ratio is negative over a 3-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth GDPC1 exceeding the historical trend rate of 2.5% signals expanding corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.2% while the federal funds rate remains below 1.5%, it indicates a labor market deterioration that the Fed will accommodate with further easing, boosting equity valuations.
failure_condition: The dual condition fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns in more than 40% of historical instances, or the signal produces a negative mean return in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production INDPRO accelerates while the unemployment rate UNRATE remains sticky above 5.0% indicates productivity-driven supply expansion without wage inflation, supporting margin growth and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The INDPRO and UNRATE divergence signal yields a negative or flat Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns do not exceed the baseline median by at least 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high inflation measured by CPIAUCSL, rising consumer credit RSAFS reflects debt-fueled consumption that becomes unsustainable once the Fed hikes, leading to a sharp equity correction.
failure_condition: Rising RSAFS during high CPI periods does not precede negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% in walk-forward validation.
